book_claims.claim: Whole ancient grain used as pasta substitute and grain base in the Longevity Diet; acceptable pasta substitute maintaining similar nutrient composition alongside farro, barley, and similar grains
book_claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrate with better nutrient profile than refined grains; lower glycemic impact

supplementary_research.finding: Spelt has a lower glycemic index and higher magnesium and zinc content than modern wheat; ancient grains associated with improved metabolic markers in intervention studies
Whole grain consumption (including spelt) inversely associated with cardiovascular mortality and type 2 diabetes risk in meta-analysis

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Mix of soluble and insoluble; supports microbiome and glycemic control
Higher than modern wheat; supports glucose metabolism and muscle function
Contains gluten; higher protein than white wheat with more complete amino acid profile

synergies.description: Ancient grain + legume combination provides complete amino acid profile; classic Mediterranean pairing
Fat lowers glycemic load of spelt dishes; traditional dressing in grain salads
Book lists spelt as an interchangeable 40g dry-weight substitute for pasta in longevity meal plans
